title: CodeRAG-Bench: Can Retrieval Augment Code Generation?
summary: This work introduces CodeRAG-Bench, a large-scale benchmark for retrieval-augmented code generation (RACG) spanning diverse tasks: general programming, open-domain, and repository-level problems. It aggregates documents from five sources (competition solutions, online tutorials, library documentation, StackOverflow, GitHub repositories) to serve as retrieval contexts. Experiments reveal that while gold-standard contexts significantly boost code generation models like GPT-4, current retrieval models often fail to fetch helpful contexts, limiting end-to-end RAG performance. The benchmark provides a unified interface for retrieval, generation, and evaluation, and aims to spur advancements in code-oriented RAG systems.
author: Zora Zhiruo Wang, Akari Asai, Xinyan Velocity Yu, Frank F. Xu, Yiqing Xie, Graham Neubig, Daniel Fried
